mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate cuts below 1.5% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This mechanism operates continuously whenever the policy rate is in the lower bound regime, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.97% on 2008-10-01 indicates emergency easing
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle at zero lower bound
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS < 1.5% is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in low-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 6.0% signal deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger flight-to-safety equity selling and reduce forward earnings expectations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when unemployment crosses above this level.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.5% on 2008-10-01 indicates labor market stress
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE > 6.0% crossings are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline more than 2% month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and forward earnings deterioration. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when this sales contraction is observed.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363696M on 2008-10-01 reflects demand weakness
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following > 2% RSAFS declines are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly decline, indicating synchronized supply-side and labor contraction. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when both series contract simultaneously, as it signals broad economic deterioration.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both decline month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.254 and PAYEMS at 136899K on 2008-10-01 show contraction
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor contraction in recession
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline are positive in > 50% of instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 800K units annualized, signaling severe demand collapse in the construction and real estate sectors. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 817K on 2008-09-01 near critical threshold
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following HOUST < 800K are positive in > 65% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) declines month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains above 0.5%, indicating deflation risk with limited policy accommodation. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market fears policy constraints during demand collapse.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 218.813 and FEDFUNDS at 0.97% on 2008-10-01
regime_hint: deflation risk with constrained monetary policy
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when both conditions are met are positive in > 55% of instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines more than 100K persons month-over-month, signaling accelerating job losses that trigger equity risk-off behavior. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as labor market deterioration cascades into earnings revisions.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136899K on 2008-10-01 with ongoing contraction
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following > 100K PAYEMS declines are positive in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) contracts quarter-over-quarter while retail sales (RSAFS) decline simultaneously, indicating synchronized demand destruction across both consumption and overall economic output. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession becomes evident.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines quarter-over-quarter and RSAFS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11720B and RSAFS at 363696M on 2008-10-01 reflect demand contraction
regime_hint: confirmed recession with synchronized demand collapse
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when both GDPC1 and RSAFS decline are positive in > 45% of instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
